claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you wanting to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll need to gather some more details to assess your eligibility.

**How old is your child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation and responsibility for Alex.

**Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to check if anyone else is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital or residential accommodation for more than 12 weeks?
- In care (arranged by the local council) for more than 8 weeks?
- Living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care arranged by the local council, but it's only been for a couple of months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important detail. I need to ask a few follow-up questions about Alex's care situation.

**Does Alex spend any regular time at home with you? For example, does Alex come home for at least 24 hours per week, or spend at least 2 nights in a row each week at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is in the care home full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Thank you for clarifying that.

**How long has Alex been in care? You mentioned a couple of months - is it more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been exactly 8 weeks.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Since Alex has been in care for exactly 8 weeks (which is the threshold), I need to ask one more important question:

**Is the local council paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs while in care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the local council is paying for Alex's accommodation and maintenance while in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- Alex is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK and are a UK citizen ✓
- You were living with Alex ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

**However**, Alex has now been in local council care for exactly 8 weeks, and the local council is paying for Alex's accommodation and maintenance.

According to the Child Benefit rules, when a child goes into care and the local council is paying for their accommodation or maintenance, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for up to 8 weeks. Since Alex has now been in care for exactly 8 weeks, you have reached the limit of eligibility.

Unless Alex returns home for at least 24 hours per week, or spends 2 nights in a row each week or 7 days or more in a row at home, you would not be eligible to continue receiving Child Benefit beyond this point.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that you're **not eligible** to continue receiving Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

If Alex's circumstances change - for example, if Alex comes home regularly (at least 24 hours per week, or 2 consecutive nights each week, or 7 days or more in a row) - you may be able to claim Child Benefit again. If that happens, you should contact HMRC to discuss your situation.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and the claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK who was living with Alex. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. However, Alex has been in local council care for exactly 8 weeks, with the local council paying for accommodation and maintenance. Under Child Benefit rules, payments can continue for up to 8 weeks when a child goes into care (where the council pays for accommodation/maintenance). Since the 8-week limit has now been reached and Alex does not spend at least 24 hours per week at home, the claimant is not eligible to continue receiving Child Benefit for Alex beyond this point.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets the basic eligibility criteria as a UK citizen responsible for an 8-year-old child. However, because Alex has been in local council care (with the council paying costs) for exactly 8 weeks, the claimant has reached the maximum period for which Child Benefit can be paid in these circumstances. Without regular home visits of sufficient duration, eligibility cannot continue.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old and the claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK who was living with Alex. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. However, Alex has been in local council care for exactly 8 weeks, with the local council paying for accommodation and maintenance. Under Child Benefit rules, payments can continue for up to 8 weeks when a child goes into care (where the council pays for accommodation/maintenance). Since the 8-week limit has now been reached and Alex does not spend at least 24 hours per week at home, the claimant is not eligible to continue receiving Child Benefit for Alex beyond this point.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets the basic eligibility criteria as a UK citizen responsible for an 8-year-old child. However, because Alex has been in local council care (with the council paying costs) for exactly 8 weeks, the claimant has reached the maximum period for which Child Benefit can be paid in these circumstances. Without regular home visits of sufficient duration, eligibility cannot continue.
